content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33511603/", "source_title": "Aging Methods-An Evaluation of Their Influence on Bond Strength", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.55, "overall_relevance": 0.25, "year": 2021, "authors": [], "venue": "European Journal of Dentistry", "doi": "10.1055/s-0040-1721906", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Thermocycling aging at 20,000 and 30,000 cycles significantly reduced the bond strength to enamel when the self-etch approach was used, while NaOCl storage increased bond strength under evaluated conditions.", "direct_quote": "Thermocycling aging (20,000 and 30,000 cycles) significantly reduced the bond strength to enamel using the SE approach. On the contrary, storage with the NaOCl method proved to increase bond strength under the evaluated conditions. Marginally relevant: demonstrates thermal cycling degradation methodology but addresses dental substrates rather than encapsulant-substrate adhesion in electronics."}, {"source_url": "https://www.colorado.edu/lab/mcgehee/sites/default/files/attached-files/encapsulating.pdf", "source_title": "Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat and thermal cycling", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.82, "overall_relevance": 0.78, "year": 2018, "authors": ["Cheacharoen R", "Boyd CC", "Burkhard GF", "Leijtens T", "Raiford JA", "Bush KA", "Bent SF", "McGehee MD"], "venue": "Sustainable Energy & Fuels", "doi": "10.1039/c8se00250a", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Glass-glass encapsulated perovskite solar cells with butyl rubber edge seal and EVA encapsulant passed the IEC 61646 standard 1000-hour damp heat test at 85 C and 85% relative humidity.", "direct_quote": "glass-glass encapsulated PSCs with a butyl rubber edge seal and an ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA) encapsulant passed the 1000 hour damp-heat test. We have used the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) 61646 standard for thin film modules to accelerate different degradation pathways that would inhibit a 25 year lifetime", "fact_category": "standard_reference", "relevance_score": 0.88, "confidence": 0.93, "perspective": "Regulatory", "entities": ["IEC 61646", "damp heat test", "1000 hours", "85 C", "85% RH", "EVA", "butyl rubber"]}, {"statement": "EVA encapsulant decomposed into acetic acid under damp heat conditions and reacted with perovskite causing visible PbI2 degradation, making it chemically unsuitable for perovskite solar cell encapsulation.", "direct_quote": "heat and moisture induced decomposition of the EVA into acetic acid, which in turn reacted with the perovskite, as also confirmed. Since EVA likely reacts chemically with perovskites in damp heat conditions and has been implicated in potential induced degradation (PID) in Si solar panels, it is not favorable for PSCs", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.91, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["EVA", "acetic acid", "perovskite", "PbI2", "PID", "damp heat"]}, {"statement": "Encapsulant elastic modulus comparison showed EVA at 10 MPa, Surlyn at 394 MPa, and polyolefin variants from 7 to 20 MPa, with lower modulus polyolefins showing best damp heat survival without delamination.", "direct_quote": "Ethylene vinyl acetate: Elastic modulus (MPa) 10. Surlyn Dupont: 394. 3M: 9.1. Polyolefin DNP CVF: 20. FirstPVM ENLIGHT: 7. Stiff Surlyn, which adheres well to glass and ITO, pulled up on the ITO and delaminated the PSCs, creating a pathway for moisture induced degradation", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.79, "confidence": 0.94, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["EVA", "Surlyn", "polyolefin", "elastic modulus", "10 MPa", "394 MPa", "7 MPa", "delamination"]}, {"statement": "Polyolefin encapsulants showed no chemical reaction with perovskite, high electrical resistivity reducing PID susceptibility, and low elastic modulus enabling thermal cycle survival between -40 C and 85 C.", "direct_quote": "polyolefin is less chemically reactive with the perovskite but cannot rule out that it functions better by being less permeable. polyolefin encapsulants have high electrical resistivity, making them less prone to PID, unlike EVA. it also has a low elastic modulus similar to EVA, which was demonstrated to withstand the 200 thermal cycle test", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.82, "confidence": 0.89, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["polyolefin", "PID", "electrical resistivity", "thermal cycling", "-40 C", "85 C"]}, {"statement": "Butyl rubber edge seal has two orders of magnitude lower water vapor transmission rate (WVTR) compared with UV-curable epoxies and polymeric encapsulants, and a 1.5 cm bond width is modeled adequate for 25-year lifetime.", "direct_quote": "butyl rubber with added desiccant and a glass transition temperature below -50 C because it can be compliant throughout temperature cycling; it is also superb in delaying moisture ingress with two orders of magnitude lower Water Vapor Transmission Rate (WVTR) compared with UV-curable epoxies and polymeric encapsulants. Kempe et al. modeled a bond width of 1.25 cm for butyl rubber with added desiccant to be adequate for 25 year lifetime", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.72, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["butyl rubber", "WVTR", "25-year lifetime", "1.25 cm bond width", "edge seal"]}], "evidence_summary": "Critical study on encapsulant selection for damp heat durability of perovskite solar cells.
